steps.title: Identify the pivotal moment
steps.content: The decisive clue is “This affectionate willingness to fall in with his own plans quite won the old man's heart.” This phrase shows that Adam's flexibility and consideration directly caused the old man's heart to open, transforming them from strangers into “almost like old friends.”

steps.title: Match the purpose to the choices
steps.content: Choice B correctly states the main purpose—Adam's flexibility and consideration won his great-uncle's heart and deepened their relationship. The other choices either focus on peripheral details (the sleeping arrangement), mischaracterize the passage's focus (excitement about the journey rather than relationship development), or introduce contrasts that the text never establishes (Richard's initial wariness). That matches choice B — B is correct.
